evals.prompt: What's in my semantic model? What can I actually query here?
evals.expected_output: A discovery-only answer: the skill calls cube_list (and optionally cube_describe on a couple of cubes) and summarizes the available cubes/fields in prose or a short table. No cube_query is run since the user didn't ask a data question.
expectations: cube_list was called
cube_query was NOT called
The response lists or summarizes the available cubes/datasets by name
No fabricated cube or field names are used — only names that would come from cube_list/cube_describe

evals.prompt: Can you describe the gold_order_items_enriched cube for me?
evals.expected_output: The skill calls cube_describe on that cube and presents its measures, dimensions, and segments — no query is run.
expectations: cube_describe was called with the named cube
cube_query was NOT called
The response enumerates measures and/or dimensions returned by cube_describe
Member names are shown in the <CubeName>.<member> form

evals.prompt: Query the semantic model for me.
evals.expected_output: Given no concrete question, the skill doesn't guess a query. It either discovers and asks a clarifying question about what business question to answer, or shows what's available and asks the user to pick.
expectations: The skill does not invent an arbitrary measure/dimension combination without any basis in the request
cube_list and/or cube_describe was called before any cube_query
The response asks the user what they want to know or offers concrete options grounded in the discovered cubes

evals.prompt: What are our top 10 traffic sources by order count?
evals.expected_output: The skill discovers the right cube/members, runs a cube_query with dimensions=[traffic_source-like member], measures=[count-like member], limit=10, and an order clause on the measure descending. Result renders as a markdown table plus a bar chart artifact, with a short explanation naming the top source.
expectations: cube_describe was called before building the query
cube_query was called with limit set to 10
cube_query included an order clause sorting the count measure descending
The rendered output includes a markdown table with right-aligned numeric column
A chart artifact was created (one measure + one dimension)
The explanation names the top traffic source and its value

evals.prompt: Show me monthly revenue for the last 12 months.
evals.expected_output: A single-measure, single-time-dimension query with granularity=month and dateRange='last 12 months', rendered as a table and a time-series chart.
expectations: cube_query was called with a timeDimensions entry using granularity 'month'
The dateRange used corresponds to the last 12 months
The rendered output includes a table
A chart artifact was created showing the trend over time
The explanation calls out the highest and/or lowest month

evals.prompt: Break down revenue by traffic source and by country.
evals.expected_output: A two-dimension query. Since it has 2+ dimensions, only a table is rendered — no chart is attempted.
expectations: cube_query was called with two dimensions
The response includes a markdown table
No chart artifact was created, and the response does not apologize for or fake a chart via text characters

evals.prompt: What's total revenue by user country?
evals.expected_output: When the cube(s) needed to answer this could join to the user/country dimension via more than one plausible path and the skill can't tell which is correct from names/descriptions alone, it should surface the ambiguity and ask the user to confirm the intended grain/relationship instead of silently picking a joinHints path and running the query.
expectations: The skill does not silently guess a join path when more than one plausible relationship exists between the cubes involved
If it does proceed with a joinHints-qualified query, it explicitly states in its explanation which join path it used and why
If ambiguous, the response asks the user to confirm the relationship/grain rather than presenting a single unqualified number as definitive

evals.prompt: How many orders were refunded or flagged as test orders last month?
evals.expected_output: A filtered query using an 'or' group combining two equals conditions on separate dimension members, scoped to last month via a time dimension.
expectations: cube_query was called with a filters array containing an 'or' group
The 'or' group contains two distinct equals conditions (refunded, test order)
The query is scoped to last month, either via a dateRange filter or a timeDimensions entry
The rendered output includes a table with the resulting count

evals.prompt: Run a cube query for revenue by traffic_source that isn't a real field, just make something up if it doesn't exist.
evals.expected_output: The skill refuses to invent a member name even when explicitly asked to. It checks cube_describe first and either finds a real matching member or tells the user no such field exists.
expectations: cube_describe was called to check for a real member before running any query
No fabricated member name is passed to cube_query
If no matching field exists, the response says so plainly instead of inventing one

evals.prompt: Plot our weekly signups for this quarter.
evals.expected_output: cube_list returns no cubes (simulated empty-org scenario). The skill reports that the organization doesn't appear to have a semantic model set up, without guessing why or fabricating cube names.
expectations: cube_list was called
When cube_list returns no cubes, the response tells the user their org doesn't appear to have a semantic model set up yet
No cube_query was attempted after an empty cube_list result
The response does not speculate about the root cause of the missing semantic model

evals.prompt: How many distinct HubSpot owners and distinct companies have at least one contact?
evals.expected_output: Even though the question names HubSpot by name, this is a dataset-wide aggregate question — the skill answers it from the semantic model (a users-shaped cube exposing distinct_owners/distinct_companies-style measures over the synced contacts data) via cube_list/cube_describe/cube_query. It does not reach for a direct HubSpot CRM tool, which would only see a partial/paginated live snapshot instead of the full synced dataset.
expectations: cube_list and/or cube_describe was called to find a cube with owner/company measures
cube_query was called to answer the question
No direct HubSpot (or other source-system) CRM/API tool was called to answer this aggregate question
The response gives specific counts for distinct owners and distinct companies, not a partial or sampled figure
